children.heading: Commission
children.label: 1 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission established by .section 2000e–4 of this title

children.heading: Covered entity
children.label: 2 Covered entity
children.text: The term “covered entity” means an employer, employment agency, labor organization, or joint labor-management committee.

children.heading: Direct threat
children.label: 3 Direct threat
children.text: The term “direct threat” means a significant risk to the health or safety of others that cannot be eliminated by reasonable accommodation.

children.heading: Employee
children.label: 4 Employee
children.text: The term “employee” means an individual employed by an employer. With respect to employment in a foreign country, such term includes an individual who is a citizen of the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “employer” means a person engaged in an industry affecting commerce who has 15 or more employees for each working day in each of 20 or more calendar weeks in the current or preceding calendar year, and any agent of such person, except that, for two years following the effective date of this subchapter, an employer means a person engaged in an industry affecting commerce who has 25 or more employees for each working day in each of 20 or more calendar weeks in the current or preceding year, and any agent of such person.

children.label: i
children.text: the United States, a corporation wholly owned by the government of the United States, or an Indian tribe; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a bona fide private membership club (other than a labor organization) that is exempt from taxation under .section 501(c) of title 26

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “illegal use of drugs” means the use of drugs, the possession or distribution of which is unlawful under the Controlled Substances Act [ et seq.]. Such term does not include the use of a drug taken under supervision by a licensed health care professional, or other uses authorized by the Controlled Substances Act or other provisions of Federal law.21 U.S.C. 801

children.heading: Drugs
children.label: B Drugs
children.text: The term “drug” means a controlled substance, as defined in schedules I through V of section 202 of the Controlled Substances Act [].21 U.S.C. 812

children.heading: Person, etc.
children.label: 7 Person, etc.
children.text: The terms “person”, “labor organization”, “employment agency”, “commerce”, and “industry affecting commerce”, shall have the same meaning given such terms in .section 2000e of this title

children.heading: Qualified individual
children.label: 8 Qualified individual
children.text: The term “qualified individual” means an individual who, with or without reasonable accommodation, can perform the essential functions of the employment position that such individual holds or desires. For the purposes of this subchapter, consideration shall be given to the employer’s judgment as to what functions of a job are essential, and if an employer has prepared a written description before advertising or interviewing applicants for the job, this description shall be considered evidence of the essential functions of the job.

children.label: A
children.text: making existing facilities used by employees readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: job restructuring, part-time or modified work schedules, reassignment to a vacant position, acquisition or modification of equipment or devices, appropriate adjustment or modifications of examinations, training materials or policies, the provision of qualified readers or interpreters, and other similar accommodations for individuals with disabilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “undue hardship” means an action requiring significant difficulty or expense, when considered in light of the factors set forth in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: the nature and cost of the accommodation needed under this chapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: the overall financial resources of the facility or facilities involved in the provision of the reasonable accommodation; the number of persons employed at such facility; the effect on expenses and resources, or the impact otherwise of such accommodation upon the operation of the facility;

children.label: iii
children.text: the overall financial resources of the covered entity; the overall size of the business of a covered entity with respect to the number of its employees; the number, type, and location of its facilities; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the type of operation or operations of the covered entity, including the composition, structure, and functions of the workforce of such entity; the geographic separateness, administrative, or fiscal relationship of the facility or facilities in question to the covered entity.
